title: Allometry exponent 0.35->0.25 (perception range trim)
notes: Perception mean -8% but wall p99 -1.1ms < noise floor. Kept for biological correctness, NOT a confident perf win. commit context: 6ef44f4

title: fast_inv_sqrt in distance/normalize hot path
notes: Stacked with T1.3 gave 48.5ms vs 48.0 baseline - flat/worse, within noise. Reverted 87022fa.

title: Rayon chunk size 64->256
notes: No budget movement, within noise. Reverted 87022fa.

title: SIMD-vectorize perception reject scan
notes: Estimated <1ms true effect, below noise floor. Not worth complexity. Do not revisit unless the perception algorithm changes shape.

title: Bevy Changed<> query filters to skip unchanged entities
notes: Double-counts with the frequency throttle (power-of-2 bucketing already skips most entities/tick). Redundant.

title: repr(align(16)) on hot components
notes: No measurable effect; SoA columns already cache-friendly.

title: Flat dense-array spatial grid
notes: Already implemented. Not an open idea.

title: Persistent thread-local scratch buffers in perception
notes: Already implemented (perception/systems.rs:42-49).

title: Parallelize L1 aggregation: Rayon owned-partition scatter replacing the serial non-empty-cell reduce
notes: FALSE POSITIVE — lab reported -2951us/-1523us but real-hardware A/B showed a REGRESSION: serial L1 ~3ms, parallel ~6ms, tripping the 50ms budget. Committed a0aa4e8 then REVERTED. Root cause: bit-identity forces output-partitioning, so each of ~167 L1 row-bands re-scans the ENTIRE L0 non-empty list -> ~O(L1_height x non_empty) work blowup that parallelism can't recover. DO_NOT_REVISIT this approach. Lab mis-measured it; see RCA in perf-hunt/rca-2026-06-24-l1.md.

title: Bound the L1 cone double-loop by inscribed circle + facing half-plane (bit-identical)
notes: Triage @500k only. Target phase REGRESSED +247us vs 91us noise (extra per-column sqrt+bound outweighs ~21% corner-cell save). dWallP99=-1078us inside wall noise 1272us — no real wall win. Candidate diff was already present in working tree at task start; measured then reverted.

title: Hoist redundant max_accel divide + defer SteeringContext build to the wander arm
notes: FULL @1M: steering dPhaseP99=-340us sits ON noise floor 337us (no reliable phase win); dWallP99=-1161us beats wall noise but worstPhaseReg=+40us and target signal indistinguishable from noise -> gate Ditch. Sound micro-op below detection bar at 1M. Reverted.
retest_resolved: median-gate-8255f67: wall -1161us beat wall noise and phase -340us sat ON the old p99 floor (337us); the median floor is ~10x smaller, so re-eligible. | RESOLVED 2026-06-25 under the median gate: re-measured and DITCH (regressed +3.09ms) again -> not re-eligible.

title: Cost-decorrelated throttle bucketing: scramble+cost-key the perception bucket so giants spread across ticks
notes: Triage @500k only. Perception REGRESSED +475us vs 91us noise (scramble+keyed-gate overhead + worse cache locality cost more than tail flattening saves). dWallP99=-78us far inside wall noise 1272us. Hypothesis not supported. Reverted.

title: Break a fork-join barrier: overlap update_body_size_cache with the perception subchain
notes: FULL @1M: behavior dPhaseP99=-220us just beats phase noise 127us but tiny; dWallP99=-777us BELOW wall noise 908us — not a credible end-to-end win. Reorder is correct (bit-identical + cache-invariant tests) but payoff vanishes into noise at scale. Reverted.
retest_resolved: median-gate-8255f67: phase win was real (-220us > 127us) but wall -777us sat below the old 908us wall floor; the median wall floor is ~124-800us, so the end-to-end win may now bank. Re-eligible. | RESOLVED 2026-06-25 under the median gate: re-measured and DITCH (+0.82ms) again -> not re-eligible.

title: Bundle (stacked DEFER union)
notes: No bundle formed this run — only one KEEP (parallel-l1-aggregation) and zero DEFERs to stack. Nothing to A/B as a union.

title: Steering avoidance: sqrt-free closing-direction pre-filter before the normalize divide
notes: Triage @500k only. Steering dPhaseP99=-113us inside phaseNoise 137us (not a real signal); dWallP99=+150us moved the wrong way (wallNoise 325us). Converging-neighbor path adds a divide it didn't have; in dense packs most in-range neighbors converge, so hot path got marginally more work — net wash buried in noise. Reverted; clean-tree guard re-passed.

title: T3.1 Software-pipeline grid rebuild + L1 aggregation to overlap the previous tick's steering/movement
notes: Triage @500k only. grid_rebuild p99 5600 vs 3653us -> dPhaseP99=+1947us REGRESSION (>> phaseNoise 63us); wall dWallP99=+4613us hard regression (wallNoise 192us). rayon::join overlap + collect_grid_snapshot Vec alloc + L1 double-buffer cost more than serial schedule; movement and grid arms contend rather than overlap at this scale. Reverted; clean-tree guard re-passed.

title: T2.2 Sated-predator skip of the L1 strategic cone scan (Golden Zone hunger gate)
notes: Triage @500k=Defer (perception dPhaseP99=-252us ~4.7sigma, dWallP99=-241us sub-noise but negative) -> escalated. FULL @1M=Ditch: perception gain collapsed to -40us vs phaseNoise 249us, dWallP99=+592us (got slightly worse, sub-noise). Honesty caveat realized: no feeding loop -> energy monotone-drains -> sated cohort thin under bench drain; instantaneous win too small to clear p99 tail at full scale. Reverted; clean-tree guard re-passed.

title: T2.5 stochastic per-entity perception phase offset (de-sync the throttle buckets, frequency-invariant)
notes: Triage @500k only. perception dPhaseP99=+78us WORSE (phaseNoise 24us); dWallP99=+240us regression (wallNoise 226us). Frequency-invariant gate cannot reduce mean cost — only reshuffles which tick a giant lands on; on this HW the reshuffle didn't flatten the p99 tail and added a hash+add per gated entity, netting slight regression. Patch needed whitespace repair before apply. Reverted; clean-tree guard re-passed.

title: De-throttle steering cost: hoist thread_rng to a seeded PRNG + skip wander RNG for exhausted creatures
notes: Triage @500k only. SLOWER on both targeted steering (+222us vs phaseNoise 83us) and wall (+1526us, order of magnitude above wallNoise 170us). xorshift jitter + per-creature is_exhausted() branch + PhysicsTick resource read didn't save over thread_rng and perturbed the hot par_iter loop. Reverted; clean-tree guard re-passed.

title: Bundle (stacked DEFER/KEEP union)
notes: No bundle formed — zero KEEPs and zero surviving DEFERs this run. All five candidates DITCHED (one after escalating to 1M). Nothing to A/B as a union.

title: L1 cone scan: per-row x-span (disc rasterization) to skip range-rejected corner cells, bit-identical
notes: Triage-only @500k (seeds 11,42,99). dPhaseP99=+17us within phaseNoise=299us (no measurable win); dWallP99=+613us > wallNoise=308us (slight regression, not clearly negative) -> triage_only, no escalation. Disc-rasterization adds per-row sqrt + bound math but the L1 cone box scan isn't the bottleneck at this radius (cells_queried p99 essentially unchanged 1277254 vs 1277263), so it only adds overhead. Reverted clean; guard passed.

title: L1 cone scan: gate expensive cell math behind the occupancy check (skip empty-cell tax)
notes: Triage-only @500k (seeds 11,42,99). dPhaseP99=+110us below phaseNoise=190us (indistinguishable from zero); dWallP99=+670us (slight wall regression, not clearly negative) -> triage_only, no escalation. The upfront get_biosignature + is_empty branch to short-circuit classify_l1_cell nets no win — classify_l1_cell is already cheap enough that the extra branch/fetch costs more than it saves. Reverted clean; guard passed.

title: L1 cone scan: reuse the L0 loop's l1_cache to drop redundant classify_l1_cell calls
notes: Triage-only @500k (seeds 11,42,99). dPhaseP99=+592us, dWallP99=+1785us (clearly POSITIVE = candidate slower; phaseNoise=70us wallNoise=368us) -> stopped at triage. The L1-classification-cache (sized for the L0 neighbor set) misses on the wider cone-swept L1 cells, so cache lookup/insert overhead exceeds the saved get_biosignature+classify_l1_cell; perception regressed. Reverted clean; guard passed.

title: Store precomputed mass in PerceptionProxy to kill per-scan powi(3) in size-domination filter
notes: Triage @500k -> escalated to 1M (clean wall at triage). @1M (seeds 11,42,99,137,2025): dPhaseP99=+400us ABOVE phaseNoise=219us -> a REAL perception regression at scale; dWallP99=-951us sits INSIDE its own 957us wall noise so the wall win is not credible. Precomputing mass at grid-build moves powi(3) out of perception but extra field load / cache pressure on the proxy hot path outweighs the saved cube. No defensible win. Reverted clean; guard passed.

title: Perception proxy SoA split: hot [x,y,radius] reject-scan stream vs cold [vx,vy,entity]
notes: Triage-only @500k (seeds 11,42,99). dPhaseP99=+102us > phaseNoise=55us (regression); dWallP99=+427us (candidate slower, not clearly negative) -> triage_only, no escalation. Hot/cold SoA split did NOT help: the extra cold-array re-index on accept plus impl-Iterator AoS reconstruction for low-freq callers outweighed any reject-scan cache-density gain at this density. Reverted clean; guard passed.

title: Drop the redundant sqrt-based FOV cull in collect_cells_sorted_fov (bitmask re-culls the same 3x3)
notes: Triage-only @500k (seeds 11,42,99). dPhaseP99=+327us, dWallP99=+1080us (clearly POSITIVE = regression; phaseNoise=78us wallNoise=224us) -> no escalation. Dropping the collect-time FOV cone cull ADDS cells back into the query set: cells_queried p99 rose ~1.278M->1.786M (+40%); the downstream bitmask re-cull (looser 72.5deg) doesn't compensate. Both perception and wall regressed. Reverted clean; guard passed.

title: Kill per-tick query.iter_mut().collect() allocation (Windows page-fault tax) via capacity-stable buffers
notes: Triage-only @500k (seeds 11,42,99). dPhaseP99=+757us > phaseNoise=285us (wrong direction); dWallP99=+2093us > wallNoise=1023us (regression, not clearly negative) -> no escalation. Pre-sized Vec::with_capacity(hwm)+extend collect regressed the fattest steering phase rather than helping. Reverted clean; guard passed.

title: T2.1 perception range hard cap (atmospheric-extinction ceiling) to collapse the giant L1-cone tail
notes: Triage @500k -> escalated to 1M (wall negative but within noise; escalated to be safe). @1M (seeds 11,42,99,137,2025): dPhaseP99=-315us within phaseNoise=767us; dWallP99=-189us within wallNoise=2604us — both tiny improvements buried far inside their own noise floors. The 450m extinction cap doesn't bound enough giant cones at this realistic-DNA population to move the p99 tail; worst-case L1 cones owning the tail already fall under/near the cap or are too rare. Functionally sound, no perf payoff. Reverted clean; guard passed.
retest_resolved: median-gate-8255f67: both improvements (-315us phase, -189us wall) were buried in HUGE old floors (767us/2604us); under the median floors (~200us) they may surface. Re-eligible, marginal. | RESOLVED 2026-06-25 under the median gate: re-measured and DITCH (+0.50ms) again -> not re-eligible.

title: Size-gated steering reaction latency (coast-on-momentum throttle for large creatures)
notes: Triage @500k (Defer) -> escalated to 1M. @1M (seeds 11,42,99,137,2025): phase verdict=Ditch (dPhaseP99=-524us vs phaseNoise=340us, ~1.5x, below tool KEEP bar + tiny +28us worst-phase regression). HOWEVER wall p99 improved -2.507ms vs wallNoise=1.707ms — a clear beyond-noise total-tick win, consistent in direction from triage (-0.938ms) to full (-2.507ms). DISCREPANCY FLAGGED: phase gate says Ditch but end-to-end wall is a real win (parallel Rayon phase boundaries can hide work that surfaces as overall tick reduction). steering_divisor=4, gate length>=5.0m to steer 1-in-4 ticks. Biological cost: giants get sluggish collision-avoidance; trophic canary (apex/grazer +-20%) required before merge. Worth a human look. Reverted clean; guard passed.
retest_resolved: median-gate-8255f67: STRONGEST candidate. Phase -524us was only 1.5x the old p99 floor (340us) so it Ditched, but the median floor is ~150us -> detects; wall -2507us is a clear win. Re-test FIRST; still needs the trophic canary before merge. | RESOLVED 2026-06-25 under the median gate: re-measured and DITCH (triage, +0.08ms) again -> not re-eligible.

title: Bundle (stacked KEEP/DEFER union)
notes: No bundle formed — zero KEEPs and zero surviving DEFERs. All 9 candidates DITCHED by the phase gate (two after escalating to 1M). Nothing to A/B as a union. Note: t26-size-gated-steering-latency is phase-Ditch but flagged a real -2.5ms wall p99 win for human review.

title: Hoist redundant max_accel divide + defer SteeringContext build into the Wandering arm
notes: Back-to-back triage A/B @500k (seeds 11,42,99, baseline-first). dPhaseMedian +161us within phaseNoise 304us (no real steering win, trends positive). Wall p99 across-seed p50 baseline 24558us vs cand 25432us = +874us regression. Bit-identical micro-op but signal does not clear the median floor; dWallP99 not clearly negative so triage_only, no escalation. Reverted clean; guard passed both sides.

title: Size-gated steering reaction latency (giants coast on momentum, re-steer 1-in-4 ticks)
notes: Triage A/B @500k (seeds 11,42,99, baseline-first). dPhaseMedian -15us WITHIN phaseNoise 45us (no measurable steering win); wall p99 across-seeds mean REGRESSED 24480->25759us (+1279us). Realistic log-normal DNA yields few giants so little work is skipped, while throttle-branch + index overhead nets a wall regression. BEHAVIOR CHANGE (not bit-identical): >=5.0m creatures get sluggish avoidance, coast up to 3 ticks; would need trophic canary before any merge. DITCH on perf. Reverted clean; guard passed.

title: Fuse update_steering_system + integrate_motion_system (kill a serial 1M collect + a full shared-column re-load)
notes: REPLICATED (dWall -3.746ms). Tool verdict Ditch at both pops is a PHASE-LABEL ARTIFACT: movement work re-accounted under steering, so steering balloons +6.0ms while movement collapses to 0. Real combined steer+integrate dropped ~2.9ms by deleting one serial 1M collect, one fork-join barrier, and a redundant shared-column reload. Wall banker improved robustly: full 1M/5seeds dWallMedian -3.355ms (>3x noise 1.056ms); wall p99 across-seeds p50 47754->45056us (-2.70ms), mean 48435->44515us (-3.92ms). cells_queried identical (2752367 vs 2752040) => determinism preserved. Bit-identical, no biology change, no canary. Cost: collapses two measured phases into one (weakens per-phase DETECT, must bank on wall median) + fat closure register pressure + dev-tools accel capture must move into closure. Overriding tool Ditch -> KEEP. HUMAN HOLD (2026-06-25): recorded as a TESTED perf win but deliberately NOT merged (see merge field). Tradeoffs being weighed: (1) PROFILING VISIBILITY LOST — steering and movement can no longer be measured as separate phases (movement folds to 0), blinding future per-phase DETECT on either; (2) MODULARITY/COUPLING COST — two previously-decoupled concerns (steering-force calc + motion integration) merge into one fat query+closure: the schedule gets SIMPLER (a 1M collect + fork-join barrier + redundant column reload deleted) but the code gets LESS modular and harder to read/test/profile each concern independently, plus register-pressure portability risk. Net: trades scheduling simplicity (gained) for code decoupling (lost).
merge: held — tested win; human deferring merge pending the profiling-visibility + two-concern coupling tradeoff

title: Fold aggregate_l1 into rebuild_spatial_grid_system + hoist the L1 cell accumulator out of the per-proxy inner loop
notes: Triage A/B @500k (seeds 11,42,99, baseline-first). dPhaseMedian +29us within phaseNoise 13us (effectively noise); dWallMedian +34us (positive, swamped by wallNoise 196us). Raw l1_aggregation p99 dipped 3102->3021us (~81us) but median delta sits in the floor and wall shows no net win. Bit-identical-by-design (hoisted running-reference variant; small unsafe cell_mut) but no measurable wall payoff at scale -- inner loop already memory-bound on the proxy stream. Reverted clean; guard passed.

title: Bundle (stacked KEEP/DEFER union)
notes: No bundle A/B run: exactly one KEEP (fuse-steering-integrate-system) and zero surviving DEFERs, so the union equals the single KEEP and needs no separate stacking test. Its standalone full-pop result (wall p99 -2.70ms, replicated -3.746ms) stands as the bankable win.

title: Cache derived allometric scalars (mass, max_speed, max_acceleration, max_force) in BodySize to kill per-entity powf/powi across steering/movement/perception
notes: Clean-tree guard passed; patch applied and built cleanly (30.6s). Triage A/B (baseline-first, back-to-back) at pop=500k, seeds 11,42,99. Verdict: VERDICT=Ditch phase=steering dPhaseMedian=+160us phaseNoise=18us dWallMedian=-32us wallNoise=166us worstPhaseP99Regression=+392us. Steering phase REGRESSED by +160us median (well above the 18us phase noise), and the wall-clock change (-32us) is buried inside the 166us wall noise -- i.e. dWall is not clearly negative. Per protocol (Ditch AND dWall not clearly negative) marked triage_only and skipped escalation. Likely cause: fattening BodySize from 2 to 6 f32 fields enlarges the component, hurting cache density in the hot steering/movement loops more than the saved powf/powi calls (which the optimizer/branch predictor already handle cheaply for the small set of DNA-derived lengths). dwall_p99_ms reported as dWallMedian (-0.032ms); the triage --verdict output does not print a separate p99 wall delta. Patch reverted cleanly; post-revert clean-tree guard passed (tree clean).

title: Size-gated steering reaction latency: giants coast on momentum, re-steer 1-in-N ticks (retest under median gate)
notes: Clean back-to-back A/B triage (baseline-first), pop=500k, seeds 11,42,99, --realistic-dna, half 5000x5000. Clean-tree guard passed; patch applied + built clean (cargo build --release --features dev-tools); reverted clean (guard passed). Triage verdict tool: VERDICT=Ditch dPhaseMedian=+18us phaseNoise=11us dWallMedian=+162us wallNoise=82us worstPhaseP99Regression=449us. From JSON: steering phase p99 REGRESSED +962us (base 6414.84us -> cand 7376.8us) with candidate steering-p99 stdDev exploding to 420us vs base 22us; wall p99 +78us (slower). The size-gate (length>=5.0m) only spares the giant cohort, which is a tiny fraction of realistic-DNA population, so the FrequencyThrottle bucketing + per-entity branch overhead added cost to the dominant small/medium cohort that steers every tick while saving almost nothing. dWallP99 positive (candidate slower) -> not clearly negative, stopped at triage per protocol. Net regression on the targeted phase; ditch.

title: Hoist redundant max_accel divide + defer SteeringContext build into the Wandering arm (retest under median gate)
notes:
Clean back-to-back A/B, baseline-first each round. Patch applied cleanly, candidate built OK (31s), reverted cleanly with clean-tree guard passing afterward.

TRIAGE 500k (seeds 11,42,99): VERDICT=Keep, dPhaseMedian=-52us, phaseNoise=12us, dWallMedian=-115us, wallNoise=50us. Steering p99 essentially flat (base 6618us -> cand 6598us). Because it was Keep (not Ditch), escalated per protocol.

ESCALATE 1M (seeds 11,42,99,137,2025): VERDICT=Ditch (exit 3). dPhaseMedian=+200us (inside phaseNoise=291us -> noise), dWallMedian=+637us (inside wallNoise=1050us -> noise), worstPhaseP99Regression=759us. Steering p99 regressed 13817us -> 14566us; wall p99 mean-of-p99s 46782us -> 49875us (dwall_p99 reported = +3.093ms, but candidate wall noise floor was elevated at 3232us, so this is noise-dominated rather than a real loss).

Interpretation: the hoist+defer-ctx change is at best neutral and at full pop leans slightly negative; no signal of a real win. The triage improvement did not hold at 1M. Reported dphase_ms/phase_noise_ms/wall_noise_ms are from the authoritative full-pop verdict; dwall_p99_ms is the mean-of-p99s wall delta (verdict's dWallMedian was +0.637ms). Tree clean after revert.

title: Break the update_body_size_cache fork-join barrier by overlapping the non-conflicting behavior pass (retest under median gate)
notes: Clean back-to-back A/B (baseline-first). Triage 500k (seeds 11,42,99): VERDICT=Defer, dPhaseMedian=-54us, dWallMedian=-53us, phaseNoise=18us, wallNoise=100us — not Ditch, so escalated. Full-pop 1M (seeds 11,42,99,137,2025): VERDICT=Ditch, dPhaseMedian=-38us (tiny behavior-phase gain, within phaseNoise=67us so not real signal), but dWallMedian=+824us against wallNoise=803us, worstPhaseP99Regression=823us. Reordering update_body_size_cache to overlap the behavior pass yields no defensible phase win and regresses wall time at scale. Patch reverted cleanly; git diff --quiet on apps/simulation/src passed (tree clean). dwall_p99_ms reflects dWallMedian (verdict tool reports median, not p99).

title: Split the giant L1 strategic-cone scan into its own work-stealing Rayon pass to balance the heavy tail
notes:
[replicated: dWall=-1.317ms] Clean back-to-back A/B, baseline-first, serial. Clean-tree guard passed; patch applied + git apply --check OK; built with cargo build --release --features dev-tools --bin latency_lab in 30.5s.

TRIAGE (500k, seeds 11,42,99): VERDICT=Keep dPhaseMedian=-532us phaseNoise=20us dWallMedian=-424us wallNoise=94us worstPhaseP99Regression=119us. Perception p99 base 4886us -> cand 4417us; wall p99 base 23846us -> cand 22580us. Not Ditch, so escalated.

FULL (1M, seeds 11,42,99,137,2025): VERDICT=Keep dPhaseMedian=-1123us phaseNoise=112us dWallMedian=-1468us wallNoise=320us worstPhaseP99Regression=-2us. Perception p99 base 9840us -> cand 8558us (-1282us, ~5x over 239us noise floor). Wall mean-of-p99s base 46156us -> cand 44280us (-1876us, reported as dwall_p99_ms). No other phase regressed (steering -540us, grid_rebuild -208us, behavior -189us, movement/l1_aggregation flat).

The change hoists the heavy/branchy L1 cone scan out of the main min_len(64) perception loop into a dedicated min_len(1) work-stealing Rayon pass over only CanStrategicVision-marked giants (perception.range >= L1_CELL_SIZE), so a handful of giants no longer straggle behind 63 cheap neighbors in a shared leaf. Marker assigned at spawn from static range (capability-marker pattern); disjoint mutable access (L1Vision in strategic_query, NeighborCache in main query) keeps both queries conflict-free in one system. Includes a guarding unit test. cells_queried unchanged (1277035->1277329 triage; 2752422->2752566 full) confirming identical work, just rebalanced. Patch reverted cleanly; post-revert clean-tree guard passed.

title: Compact perception to this tick's throttle bucket before the parallel dispatch
notes: [replicated: dWall=-2.444ms] Clean back-to-back A/B, baseline-first, serial. Tree clean before apply; patch applied + git-apply --check OK; candidate built OK (cargo build --release --features dev-tools --bin latency_lab, 30.6s). Triage (500k, seeds 11,42,99): VERDICT=Keep dPhaseMedian=-1314us dWallMedian=-1431us phaseNoise=76us wallNoise=470us. Escalated to full pop (1M, seeds 11,42,99,137,2025): VERDICT=Keep dPhaseMedian=-2694us dWallMedian=-2302us phaseNoise=108us wallNoise=683us worstPhaseP99Regression=1084us. dwall_p99_ms/dphase_ms reported as the gate's median-delta wall/phase metrics (the verdict tool emits median deltas, which are robust to tail noise). NOTE: the cross-seed p99 aggregate is contaminated by a single anomalous seed (2025): cand perception p99=14.7ms and wall p99=64.8ms vs the other four seeds' perception p99 7.1-7.5ms (BETTER than baseline ~10ms) and wall p99 43.7-46.2ms. Computed raw cross-seed p99 deltas were therefore +14.46ms wall / +3.96ms perception, entirely driven by that one outlier. All 5 seeds improved at median (perception ~6.7ms vs ~9.4ms baseline; wall ~43ms vs ~45ms) and 4/5 improved at p99, so the spike reads as a one-off scheduler/system event, not a candidate property. The optimization is sound and order/Entity-preserving (bit-identical neighbor/L1Vision output by construction): it compacts the throttle+active gate into the serial collect, shrinking the dispatched parallel Vec by ~perception_divisor and removing no-op work units. Recommend a confirmation re-run to rule out the seed-2025 tail spike before merging. Patch reverted; clean-tree guard passed (tree clean).
merge: merged (c9fe2a2, branch perf/perception-compact-active-set); human-validated 10-min soak at 1M held ~46-48ms total tick under budget, live perception p99 ~10.6->7.45ms. RE-BASELINE the perf-hunt: perception detect bars have shifted.

title: Drop the unused HomePosition column from the 1M steering collect
notes: [NOT REPRODUCED on replication: DEFER, dWall=-1.973ms] Clean back-to-back A/B (baseline first each round). Clean-tree guard passed; patch applied + git apply --check OK; candidate built clean (29.93s). Triage @500k (seeds 11,42,99): VERDICT=Keep dPhaseMedian=-162us phaseNoise=5us dWallMedian=-122us wallNoise=35us. Escalated @1M (seeds 11,42,99,137,2025): VERDICT=Keep dPhaseMedian=-497us phaseNoise=107us dWallMedian=-464us wallNoise=217us worstPhaseP99Regression=73us. Both phase (-0.497ms) and wall (-0.464ms) deltas clear their noise floors and are negative (improvement). Dropping the unused HomePosition column from SteeringQuery shrinks the queried archetype tuple. Reverted cleanly; post-revert clean-tree guard passed. Reported dwall is dWallMedian (verdict tool emits median, not p99).

title: Atmospheric-extinction perception range hard cap to collapse the giant L1-cone tail (marginal retest under median gate)
notes:
Clean back-to-back A/B (baseline-first both rounds). Clean-tree guard passed; on-disk patch was a stale divergent version (defined MAX_PERCEPTION_RANGE in constants/perception.rs) so I overwrote it with the prompt's exact diff (inline const in perception/components.rs). Patch applied cleanly, built OK in 30s.

TRIAGE 500k (seeds 11,42,99): VERDICT=Ditch, dPhaseMedian=+46us (phaseNoise 18us), dWallMedian=-22us (wallNoise 20us). dWallP99 across-seeds = -553us, which is "clearly negative" — but that was an artifact of one slow baseline seed (base wall p99 stdDev=530us vs cand 16us). Since dWallP99 was clearly negative, the early-out condition did NOT trigger, so I escalated per protocol.

ESCALATE 1M (seeds 11,42,99,137,2025): VERDICT=Ditch. dPhaseMedian=+39us (a slight REGRESSION) against phaseNoise=205us — perception phase did NOT improve; perception p99 mean actually rose 10225.8->10420.2us. dWallMedian=-511us but wallNoise=734us; dWallP99=+502us. Wall deltas flip sign between median and p99 and are all well inside noise — pure jitter, no real wall movement. worstPhaseP99Regression=194us.

Conclusion: the atmospheric-extinction cap (450m) produces no measurable perception-phase win at the realistic-DNA distribution — evidently very few/no giant narrow-FOV cones exceed 450m in this population, so the cap rarely fires and the worst-case L1 cone tail is unchanged. Reverted cleanly; post-revert clean-tree guard passed.

title: Sub-throttle the L1 strategic cone scan below the L0 tactical cadence (giant-cone p99 tail)
notes: Clean-tree guard passed. Patch had a cosmetic hunk line-count mismatch in the provided diff (last hunk header @@ -499,7 +516,7 @@ declared 7 context lines but only 6 were present); applied/reverted with `git apply --recount`, which validated and applied cleanly. Candidate built OK (release, dev-tools, latency_lab). Triage (500k, seeds 11,42,99) = Defer: dPhaseMedian=-49us/noise=10us, dWallMedian=-231us/noise=232us — wall within noise but phase slightly positive and dWall negative, so escalated per protocol. Full-pop (1M, seeds 11,42,99,137,2025) = Ditch: dPhaseMedian=-134us vs phaseNoise=130us (improvement barely at the noise floor), dWallMedian=-798us vs wallNoise=846us (wall improvement entirely inside noise), worstPhaseP99Regression=-109us (no regression). Note: the verdict tool reports dWallMedian (no separate wall p99); dwall_p99_ms here is the reported dWallMedian. Direction is favorable but the gain is not separable from noise at full pop, hence Ditch. Sub-throttling the L1 strategic cone to half cadence does shave the perception giant-cone work, but the saving is too small to clear the noise bar. Reverted with `git apply -R --recount`; post-revert clean-tree guard PASSED (tree clean).

title: Bundle (stacked KEEP union)
notes: No separate bundle A/B run. Two KEEPs this run, both targeting perception and both replicated: perception-l1-cone-parallel-split (wall p99 -1.876ms, replicated -1.317ms) and perception-compact-active-set-before-dispatch (wall p99 -2.302ms median, replicated -2.444ms). Zero surviving DEFERs. A stacking test of the two perception KEEPs together was NOT executed by the workflow (bundle=null); they touch the same perception system/dispatch path so additivity is NOT proven -- recommend an explicit A/B of the stacked pair before merging both, since they may compete for the same dispatch/bandwidth saving rather than add. Standalone full-pop results stand as the individually-banked wins.

title: Stack A/B: perception cone-split + compact-active-set (additivity test)
notes: Same-session back-to-back A/B at 1M/5-seed through the median gate (diffs recovered from the run journal, hand-merged: both edit update_perception_system). Wall median: cone-split -1.49ms, compact -2.57ms, STACK -2.99ms vs -4.06ms naive sum = 74% additive (perception phase 86%). NOT one-or-the-other (stack beats best single by 0.43ms) but heavily sub-additive -- they share the perception dispatch path. Compact is the keystone: captures 86% of the stack alone, 1 file, no new component. Cone-split marginal value collapses to -0.43ms when stacked on compact (vs -1.49ms standalone), for much more complexity (CanStrategicVision marker + split query + 2nd fork-join + static-range invariant). cells_queried identical 2110962->2110565 => determinism preserved. RECOMMENDATION: merge compact; cone-split is a weak add-on. Both still merge=held. Side-note: test_fov_variants_medium_90_crowd is a pre-existing flake (~1/6 on main too).

title: Phase-visible recovery of the held fuse: one shared 1M collect, two separately-timed par_iter passes
notes: TRIAGE-ONLY (500k, seeds 11,42,99), baseline-first back-to-back serial. Patch applied/built (31s)/reverted clean; tree guard passed post-revert. Verdict: Ditch phase=steering dPhaseMedian=-1926us (phaseNoise=13us) dWallMedian=+482us (wallNoise=111us) worstPhaseP99Regression=295us. The fuse genuinely shrinks the targeted phases: triage steering p99 6479->4724us (-1.76ms) and movement 4227->2904us (-1.32ms), ~3ms saved across the two fused passes from deleting the redundant 1M gather+reload. But wall p99 REGRESSED: base 21710us vs cand 23682us; candidate wall noise much higher (std 1815us vs 375us). Per-phase savings did NOT propagate to end-to-end latency, so per protocol (Ditch AND dWallP99 not clearly negative) = triage-only DITCH, no escalation. Recovers the collect+reload but RETAINS the fork-join barrier, so it isolates that the BARRIER (not the collect) was the real cost of the held -2.7ms fuse.

title: Replace serial 1M collect()+rayon with Bevy native par_iter_mut() in steering + movement
notes: BUILD FAILED (cargo exit 101). Candidate diff references bevy_ecs::query::BatchingStrategy which does not exist at that path in bevy_ecs 0.14.2 — error[E0433] at steering/system.rs:170 and movement/systems.rs:62. (In 0.14 the type is re-exported elsewhere, not bevy_ecs::query::BatchingStrategy.) Clean-tree guard passed before apply; patch applied and reverted cleanly (git apply -R exit 0, post-revert tree clean). No measurement performed.

title: Movement: drop transcendental powf in turn-rate base (exponent==1.0) and quartic max_speed
notes: Clean A/B baseline-first back-to-back. Tree clean before apply; built 30s; reverted clean, guard passed. Triage (500k, 11,42,99): Defer dPhaseMedian=-340us phaseNoise=14us dWallMedian=-123us wallNoise=97us — escalated. Full (1M, 5 seeds): Defer dPhaseMedian=-272us phaseNoise=124us dWallMedian=+703us wallNoise=521us worstPhaseP99Regression=1024us. Movement-phase win is real and consistent (-340us then -272us, well above phase noise) — sqrt().sqrt() for powf(0.25) and identity for powf(1.0) cut movement time. But wall p99 REGRESSED +703us against 521us noise (~1.35x, not clearly real either way): phase-local win does not carry to wall. TRADEOFF: turn-rate divide is bit-identical, zero risk; the max_speed sqrt.sqrt half perturbs ~1 ULP and needs the determinism canary before merging that half. Stacks cleanly with atan2-free turn-limit (same loop).

title: Golden-Zone: giants ignore mice — size-domination early-out in the avoidance neighbor loop
notes: TRIAGE-ONLY. Prompt patch was truncated (lost leading-space on 3 blank context lines + missing final trailing context line; header 11/15 vs body 10/14). Repaired both; git apply --check passed, built 30.6s. Triage A/B (baseline-first, 11,42,99, 500k, realistic-dna): Ditch phase=steering dPhaseMedian=-50us (phaseNoise=51us) dWallMedian=-125us (wallNoise=118us) — both deltas at/under the noise floor, no measurable win; worstPhaseP99Regression=71us. dWall not clearly negative so triage-only, escalation skipped. Reverted; guard passed. TRADEOFF: BEHAVIOR CHANGE (not bit-identical) — large creatures stop deflecting around much smaller ones (intended size-dominance/trample), removes survival buffer for smallest grazers; RATIO is a magic number to migrate to DNA. Ledger #15 (avoidance-sqrt-free) warns such per-neighbor branches WASH in dense convergent packs — confirmed here; win only materializes with real size disparity. If revisited: must run --realistic-dna + TROPHIC CANARY (apex & grazer populations within +-20%) and log gene/ratio in biology-notes.md.

title: Movement: atan2-free turn-rate limiting via dot/cross (kill 2 fast_atan2 per moving entity)
notes: Clean back-to-back A/B baseline-first serial. Guard passed pre-apply; git apply --check OK; built 29.97s; reverted clean. TRIAGE 500k (11,42,99): Keep dPhaseMedian=-344us phaseNoise=27us dWallMedian=-438us wallNoise=95us — escalated. FULL 1M (5 seeds): Defer dPhaseMedian=-347us phaseNoise=118us dWallMedian=-506us wallNoise=683us worstPhaseP99Regression=397us. Movement phase is a real repeatable win (~0.35ms at both pops, well above 0.118ms phase noise). Wall improvement (-0.506ms) is SMALLER than wall noise (0.683ms), not distinguishable from drift at full pop => Defer not Keep. Replaces 2 atan2 on turn-limit path with dot/cross + single sincos only on the clamp branch; ships unit tests proving limit_turn matches old atan2 within 1e-4 and leaves velocity untouched on the no-clamp path. TRADEOFF: NOT bit-identical (clamped headings via direct rotation) — needs determinism canary (cells_queried identical + heading distribution within tolerance across 5 seeds) before merge; not a trophic canary (intent unchanged).

title: Bundle (stacked DEFER union: kill-powf + atan2-free turn-limit)
notes: UNION COLLAPSED — only 1 of 2 patches stacked. kill-powf-and-quartic-speed applied cleanly; atan2-free-turn-limit was SKIPPED: it conflicts with the already-applied patch (git apply --check AND --3way both fail). They fight over the SAME source — both rewrite the turn-rate-limiter block in integrate_motion_system (movement/systems.rs ~L160-196) and both edit the math import (L9). atan2-free was authored against the clean baseline (its hunk context still carries the powf(TURN_RATE_SIZE_EXPONENT) line kill-powf deletes), so it cannot machine-merge onto the kill-powf tree; combining needs a hand-merge. The conflict IS the answer: these two defers are mutually exclusive as authored — the strongest possible fight over the same resource. A/B (baseline-first, union=patch1-only, full 5-seed 1M): Defer dPhaseMedian=-296us (movement ~0.30ms faster, ~2.4x its 124us phase noise — real but small) dWallMedian=+143us vs wallNoise=553us — wall p99 did NOT improve outside noise (raw 45293us base vs 47068us union). worstPhaseP99Regression=1016us. Bank bar NOT cleared. Guard passed start; reversed; final git diff --quiet -- apps/simulation/src CLEAN. Artifacts: bundle.base.json, bundle.full.json in target/perf-hunt/.

title: Hand-merged movement turn-limiter: kill identity powf(1.0) AND atan2-free dot/cross clamp in one rewrite
notes: Clean A/B at 500k triage, baseline-first then candidate, serial. Patch needed --recount to apply: the supplied diff's last hunk header (-156,38 +157,40) overcounted by one context line vs the actual body (ctx=5, removed=32, added=34); content matched the diff exactly, only the header counts were stale, so --recount applied/reverted it faithfully. Also had to restore leading spaces on 4 blank context lines that the editor stripped. Build OK in 34s. Triage verdict=Ditch: dPhaseMedian=-199us (movement phase faster, phaseNoise=111us — a real but tiny ~0.2ms phase win) but dWallMedian=+321us (candidate SLOWER at wall, wallNoise=870us so within noise), and worstPhaseP99Regression=493us. dWall is positive, not clearly negative, so triage_only=true and escalation skipped per protocol. Note: verdict tool reports dWallMedian, not dWallP99 — value reported in dwall_p99_ms field is the median wall delta (0.321ms). The atan2-free turn limiter shaves a little off the movement phase but does not move total wall time favorably; net no win. Reverted; clean-tree guard passed (exit 0).

title: Skip the provably-no-op acceleration cap for pure wanderers and catatonic-no-neighbor entities
notes: Clean A/B at 500k triage (seeds 11,42,99, baseline-first then candidate, back-to-back). Tree was clean before apply; patch applied + built clean (35.4s); reverted clean after (git diff --quiet passed). Triage verdict=Ditch: the steering phase REGRESSED by +574us (dPhaseMedian, phaseNoise only 12us so well outside noise) and wall REGRESSED by +1011us (dWallMedian, wallNoise 402us). dWall is clearly POSITIVE (a regression), not negative, so triage_only=true and escalation was correctly skipped. The added per-entity branches (avoidance_fired hoist, Catatonic early-out, and the conditional cap gate with its matches! on behavior) cost more than the elided max_accel recompute + cap saves — the cap_acceleration path the diff hoped to skip is evidently cheap/branch-predicted, while the new conditionals add work on the hot per-creature closure. No behavioral test failures observed (build succeeded incl. the new invariant test). Verdict binary exit code 3 = Ditch. dwall_p99_ms field carries dWallMedian (1.011ms) since the verdict tool reported median deltas, not p99.

title: Compute max_accel from the cached inv_sqrt_length instead of max_force()/mass()
notes: Clean A/B at 500k triage (seeds 11,42,99). Verdict tool: VERDICT=Ditch phase=steering dPhaseMedian=+39us (phaseNoise=31us) dWallMedian=+119us (wallNoise=47us) worstPhaseP99Regression=104us. The candidate is SLOWER, not faster: wall p99 went 22983.92us->23284.72us (dWallP99=+0.301ms), steering phase p99 6531.6us->6708.64us (+0.177ms, but candidate stddev 116us vs base 43us so within noise/drift). dWallP99 is clearly POSITIVE (not negative), so per protocol triage_only=true and escalation skipped. The strength-reduction (BASE_ACCELERATION*inv_sqrt_length vs max_force()/mass()) is algebraically sound but produced no measurable steering win at this pop — the two max_force()/mass() call sites likely already fold to the same cached math under -O3 (or steering is not arithmetic-bound here; the phase is dominated by neighbor cache traversal in the avoidance branch). Build clean (34.74s), candidate unit test included in diff. Patch applied and reverted cleanly; final git diff --quiet on apps/simulation/src PASSED (tree clean).

title: Replace the per-perceiver atan2 octant classification with branchless sign/ratio compares
notes: [NOT REPRODUCED on replication: DEFER, dWall=-0.317ms] Clean back-to-back A/B (baseline-first each round), serial. Tree clean before apply; patch applied + built (35s, release+dev-tools); reverted cleanly after, clean-tree guard passed. Replaces atan2-based facing_to_octant with a branchless sign/ratio classifier (octant_branchless) in the perception hot loop; includes an exhaustive 0.5deg sweep equivalence test vs the atan2 reference so cells_queried stays bit-identical (confirmed: cells_queried p99 ~2752386 both arms, within noise). TRIAGE 500k/3 seeds: VERDICT=Keep, dPhaseMedian=-245us (phaseNoise=31us), dWallMedian=-341us (wallNoise=81us), worstPhaseP99Regression=63us. ESCALATED 1M/5 seeds: VERDICT=Keep, dPhaseMedian=-761us (phaseNoise=150us), dWallMedian=-1479us (wallNoise=189us), worstPhaseP99Regression=45us. Perception p99 dropped 8885us->7517us; wall p99 47539us->45616us. dWall well beyond wall noise floor; clean detectable win with no phase regressions above noise.

title: Fuse behavior_transition into steering (kill a serial 1M collect + a fork-join barrier, keep movement visible)
notes: Escalated to full pop (1M, 5 seeds). Lab tool VERDICT=Ditch on phase=steering because this is a FUSION candidate: the standalone behavior_transition_system is folded into the top of update_steering_system, so the behavior phase collapses to 0 (full-pop p99 4547us->0) and that work reappears inside the steering phase. By the steering-phase metric alone this looks like a regression (dPhaseMedian +1.284ms, phaseNoise 0.070ms, worstPhaseP99Regression 1.195ms), which is why the phase-targeted verdict mechanically returns Ditch. But the wall clock is the real signal and it is a CLEAN WIN: wall p99 mean-of-p99s 44635us->40948us, dWallMedian -2.576ms vs wallNoise only 0.114ms (>20x noise floor, unambiguous). Combined steering+behavior p99 13738+4547=18285us baseline vs 14933+0=14933us candidate = ~3.35ms saved by removing one serial collect + one fork-join barrier; bit-identical output preserved. Triage (500k/3 seeds) agreed: phase Ditch (dPhaseMedian +558us) with dWallMedian -816us, so I escalated rather than skipping because dWall was clearly negative. NOTE FOR ORCHESTRATOR: the DITCH verdict is the phase-targeted tool output as required, but it is misleading for a fusion candidate -- by wall p99 this is a genuine ~2.6-3.7ms improvement and is effectively a KEEP candidate; the parent should weigh the wall win, not the steering-phase number. Patch required git apply --recount (provided hunk header counts were stale; context matched the tree). Tree reverted clean, post-revert guard passed.

title: Native Bevy par_iter_mut for steering+movement to delete the serial 1M collect (re-author the build-errored idea)
notes: [replicated: dWall=-7.345ms] Clean back-to-back A/B, baseline-first, serial. Tree clean before apply; patch applied + git apply --check OK; candidate built OK (cargo build --release --features dev-tools --bin latency_lab, 31.8s). TRIAGE @500k: steering p99 6528->4550us, wall p99 21656->18760us; verdict KEEP dPhaseMedian=-1995us dWallMedian=-2842us phaseNoise=30us wallNoise=97us. Clearly-negative dWall -> escalated. FULL @1M (seeds 11,42,99,137,2025): steering p99 13828->9702us, movement 9361->7183us, wall p99 44268->37406us, wall mean 42642->34856us. FULL VERDICT=Keep phase=steering dPhaseMedian=-4230us(-4.23ms) dWallMedian=-7258us(-7.258ms) phaseNoise=122us(0.122ms) wallNoise=390us(0.390ms) worstPhaseP99Regression=147us (within grid_rebuild/l1 noise floors). Replacing the iter_mut().collect() -> Vec gather with native Bevy par_iter_mut (multi_threaded feature + ComputeTaskPool init, SingleThreaded system executor for determinism) kills the per-tick 1M-entity Vec allocation in BOTH steering and movement; wall win (7.26ms) exceeds steering phase win (4.23ms) because movement also shed its gather. Big, well above noise. Patch reverted cleanly; post-revert tree guard passed. Caveat: 1M baseline wall p99 noise was elevated (1222us) but candidate-vs-baseline median deltas dwarf it.

title: Golden-Zone: cap tracked neighbors 7->5 (bounded attention) — shrinks perception tail AND avoidance sqrt loop
notes: Clean A/B at triage (500k, seeds 11,42,99, baseline-first then candidate, serial). Verdict tool (--phase steering): VERDICT=Ditch, dPhaseMedian=+54us, phaseNoise=34us, dWallMedian=-162us, wallNoise=99us, worstPhaseP99Regression=154us. Reported fields use the p99-consistent direct deltas: steering p99 candidate 6724us vs baseline 6769us = -45us (within ~88us noise floor); wall p99 candidate 22810us vs baseline 22746us = +64us, i.e. NOT clearly negative (deep inside the 0.47-0.72ms wall p99 noise). Per protocol step 4, triage Ditch + dWallP99 not clearly negative => triage_only=true, escalation skipped. No measurable steering win: the 7->5 neighbor cap trims the avoidance sqrt-normalize loop but the closest neighbors already dominate the steering force, and any micro-saving is buried in noise. Behaviour-changing (not bit-identical) with zero proven perf payoff -> ditch. Build OK (release+dev-tools, 35s). Patch applied and reverted cleanly; post-revert git diff --quiet on apps/simulation/src passed (tree clean).

title: Lower steering Rayon min_batch_size to work-steal the variable avoidance tail
notes: [NOT REPRODUCED on replication: DITCH, dWall=0.657ms] Lowering steering's par_iter_mut min_len from 256 to 64 lets idle Rayon workers steal the heavy-tailed avoidance straggler tail, ending the fork-join barrier sooner. Clean A/B, baseline-first back-to-back at each stage, tree clean before apply and after revert (both guards passed). Triage @500k: VERDICT=Ditch on median (dPhaseMedian=-90us vs phaseNoise=112us, within noise) BUT wall p99 dropped clearly (26431->23249us, dWallMedian=-312us clearly negative), so per the escalation gate I escalated rather than skipping. Full-pop @1M (5 seeds): VERDICT=Keep, dPhaseMedian=-492us (steering p99 14373->13621us), dWallMedian=-2218us, worstPhaseP99Regression=-201us (no phase regressed), phaseNoise=148us and wallNoise=801us both well below the gains. Signal is clean and well above noise at 1M. Patch reverted; tree verified clean.

title: Replace powf(0.25)/powf(0.5) in BodySize allometric helpers with sqrt chains (hits steering ctx build + movement)
notes: Clean-tree guard passed; patch applied and built cleanly (cargo build --release --features dev-tools --bin latency_lab, 33.7s). Triage A/B (baseline-first, 500k, seeds 11,42,99): steering p99 6763us->6635us, wall p99 22304us->21853us. Verdict tool (--phase steering): VERDICT=Ditch dPhaseMedian=-92us phaseNoise=148us dWallMedian=-380us wallNoise=526us worstPhaseP99Regression=302us. Steering delta (-0.092ms) is within phase noise (0.148ms) and wall delta is within wall noise (0.526ms) — not a real win, and dWall p99 is not clearly negative beyond noise. Did NOT escalate to full pop per triage-Ditch rule. Patch reverted via git apply -R; post-revert git diff --quiet -- apps/simulation/src passed (tree clean). Note: the swap from powf(0.5)/powf(0.25) to sqrt()/sqrt().sqrt() is algebraically exact and the added correctness test is sound, but max_acceleration/max_speed are evidently not hot enough during the steering phase at this scale for the powf->sqrt substitution to register above measurement noise.

title: Golden-Zone: reaction-time refractory on the avoidance sub-force only (recompute every other tick, reuse last vector)
notes: Triage A/B at 500k, seeds 11,42,99, baseline-first back-to-back. Verdict tool: VERDICT=Ditch phase=steering dPhaseMedian=+171us phaseNoise=178us dWallMedian=-20us wallNoise=651us worstPhaseP99Regression=-130us. The targeted steering phase REGRESSED +171us (within 178us noise) — the opposite of what halving the avoidance sub-force should produce, so there is no real per-phase win. Robust wall delta (median) is -20us, i.e. flat against 651us noise. The wallP99-across-seeds delta of -2.107ms (base mean 25183us vs cand 23076us) is a baseline-noise artifact: the baseline triage run had one spiking seed (grid_rebuild noise 1081us vs candidate 183us; wall p99 max 28872us vs candidate 23963us), inflating base p99. Since the wall change is not credibly/clearly negative (median flat, target phase regressed), set triage_only=true and skipped full-pop escalation per protocol. PATCH NOTE: full patch failed git apply --check only in docs/biology/biology-notes.md (patch context used ASCII arrows/dashes where the live file has Unicode arrows/dashes); this is doc-only and does not affect the binary. Applied/built/measured/reverted the apps/simulation/src hunks (which applied cleanly); build OK in 35.5s. Revert clean, clean-tree guard passed.

title: No bundle assembled this run
notes: Bundle was null: only one KEEP this run (native-par-iter-kill-1m-collect, dWall p99 -7.258ms, replicated -7.345ms). A single keep needs no additivity check, so no stacked bundle A/B was run. The fuse-behavior-transition-into-steering candidate is a wall-clock win (-2.576ms) that the phase-targeted verdict tool mechanically scored DITCH because the folded behavior phase reappears inside steering; flagged for human review as a de-facto KEEP. Note both architectural collect-killers (native par_iter and the fuse) attack the SAME serial 1M collect + fork-join barrier, so they are not independently additive — pick one.

title: Pre-filter behavior_transition to throttle bucket before par_iter_mut dispatch (compact-active-set pattern)
notes: [replicated: dWall=-4.951ms] Full-pop (1M, 5 seeds) KEEP. Behavior phase -2.375ms vs noise 0.078ms (30x SNR); wall p99 -4.882ms vs noise 2.020ms (2.4x SNR). Pre-filtering throttled entities before dispatch removes ~(behavior_divisor-1)/behavior_divisor no-op Rayon work units per tick. Freed CPU headroom also improves perception/steering/movement phases, explaining wall improvement well beyond the phase saving alone. Triage also KEEP. Replicated. Mutually exclusive with fuse-behavior-transition-into-steering.

title: Fuse behavior_transition_system into the steering par_iter_mut closure (kill serial 1M collect + fork-join barrier)
notes: Strong signal buried under baseline machine noise. At 1M: behavior phase eliminated entirely (4874us->0) and wall p99 dropped ~7.7ms raw (36503->28840us). dWallMedian=-5.681ms but wallNoise=3.396ms driven by baseline variance (std=3366us vs candidate std=703us). Candidate itself is very stable. Triage (500k) showed KEEP (-2.024ms wall/-1.656ms phase). DEFER on wall noise floor alone; re-measure when baseline is stable. Mutually exclusive with behavior-compact-active-set.

title: Skip stopped catatonic creatures from spatial grid rebuild via GridFixed ZST marker
notes: Triage (500k) looked promising (dPhase=-0.237ms, dWall=-2.755ms) but full-pop (1M, 5 seeds) flipped to clear regression: dPhase=+0.364ms, dWall=+1.168ms (both above noise). Extra lifecycle systems plus cache Vec+retain() scan overhead outweighs gain from excluding stopped-catatonic entities; catatonic fraction is too small at 1M realistic-dna to offset overhead.

title: Precompute min_perceivable_radius per perceiver; reject sub-threshold proxies before dx/dy/FOV computation
notes: Triage-only (500k, 3 seeds). Perception p99 regressed +566us worst; dPhaseMedian=+96us, dWallMedian=+96us. cbrt() precomputation and per-proxy radius compare add overhead that outweighs saved dx/dy/FOV work; the prefilter rarely fires because most proxies in realistic-dna are not tiny (insufficient size diversity at benchmark time).

title: Skip near-stationary proxies beyond near-field safety zone in L0 scan (freezing = camouflage)
notes: Triage DITCH. Perception phase regressed +48us vs 30us noise; dWallMedian=-95us sub-noise (387us floor). Stationary cohort too small in realistic-dna to offset per-proxy overhead. Same failure mode as ledger #50 (inactive-cohort too thin). Trophic canary not reached.

title: Guard avoidance block with is_active() check in steering to skip provably-wasted work
notes: Escalated to full 1M/5-seed (triage showed -1.254ms wall). At 1M: dPhaseMedian=+0.779ms vs noise 1.061ms, dWallMedian=+1.921ms vs noise 2.005ms. Both within noise. Inactive creature fraction too small at realistic-dna to produce measurable signal at either pop tier. Logically sound (movement zeros accel anyway) but economically invisible.

title: Eliminate sqrt in collect_cells_sorted_fov FOV check via squared dot comparison
notes: Full-pop (1M, 5 seeds). Perception delta -0.052ms within phaseNoise 0.172ms (SNR ~0.3x). Two saved transcendentals affect only ~10 cells per creature query -- unmeasurable. Three-branch squared-comparison restructure introduces extra branching correlating with worstPhaseP99Regression=+0.492ms. Wall delta -0.049ms vs wallNoise 1.139ms is pure noise.

title: Replace unbounded NEIGHBOR_CANDIDATES Vec with fixed K-slot max-heap buffer to eliminate the final partial sort
notes: Triage-only (500k). Perception phase regressed +766us worst / +189us median vs 18us noise. Wall -302us vs 298us floor -- not clearly negative; escalation skipped. Fixed-size k-buf with O(K) worst-slot scan is slower than thread-local Vec + lazy partial-sort: per-insertion scan over K=7 exceeds savings from eliminating the deferred sort at this density.

title: Halve L1 strategic cone scan radius for hungry creatures (tunnel-vision hunger as a Golden Zone win)
notes: Triage (500k, 3 seeds). Perception p99 regressed +805us; wall p99 regressed +1156us. dPhaseMedian=0us -- no median perception improvement. Per-creature energy branch in hot parallel L1 scan adds overhead that cancels savings from scanning fewer cells. Hungry cohort insufficient to offset cost. Same root failure as ledger #17 (sated cohort too thin at bench time).

title: Hoist repeated max_force/mass/max_speed to local variables at closure top in update_steering_system
notes: Triage DITCH (500k). Wall p99 regressed +1.125ms; steering phase p99 regressed +471us worst. dPhaseMedian=+45us within noise 70us. LLVM under -O3 already CSEs these fully-inlined methods. Consistent with ledger #36 and #55: steering is not arithmetic-bound at scale.

title: Sort non-empty L0 cells by L1 parent index before aggregation loop to improve write locality
notes: Massive regression at 500K: l1_aggregation p99 jumped 3403->19033us (+15630us worst). sort_unstable_by_key over all non-empty L0 cells dominates cost at this scale. Sorting ~3.4M comparisons costs far more than any cache-locality gain. STOPPED at triage; did not escalate. Same lesson as ledger #9 (parallel-l1-aggregation): L1 data structures are memory-bandwidth-bound, not CPU-bound -- structural transforms add cost.

title: Replace dist_sq.sqrt().recip() with fast_inv_sqrt in L1 cone scan direction normalization
notes: Full-pop (1M, 5 seeds). Raw p99 deltas look promising (wall -2.274ms, perception -0.831ms) but median-level: dPhaseMedian=154us vs phaseNoise=214us -- below noise floor. dWallMedian=-405us vs wallNoise=727us -- equally inconclusive. Compiler already lowers recip() to efficient reciprocal or bottleneck is elsewhere. Consistent with ledger #2 (fast_inv_sqrt on hot path).

title: Bundle (2026-06-27 run -- no bundle A/B run)
notes: Bundle was null per run config. One KEEP (behavior-compact-active-set, wall p99 -4.882ms replicated -4.951ms) and one DEFER (fuse-behavior-transition-into-steering, wall p99 -5.681ms buried in baseline noise). These two are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE (both target behavior_transition_system -- implement exactly one). No stacking test applicable. Recommendation: merge behavior-compact-active-set now; re-measure fuse candidate when machine baseline stabilizes.

title: Cache cell index in the count phase, reuse it in scatter (compute the counting-sort key once, not twice)
notes: cloud triage pop=10000 seeds=11,42,99 (shared VM): grid_rebuild p99 1020us->864us, Δphase -0.053ms (phaseNoise 4us, beyond noise); wall p99 3126us->2871us, Δwall -0.077ms (wallNoise 23us); worstPhaseP99Regression 27us. Function bench Δ 0% (UNAVAILABLE — criterion simulation_bench fails to compile on a PRE-EXISTING, unrelated bug: benches/simulation_bench.rs:199 passes a 6-tuple to rebuild_parallel which expects 7). Growth exponent b 0.361->0.331 (modest scaling-class improvement). Caches the flat cell index from the count pass and reuses it in scatter, dropping the redundant floor/index recompute. Clean-tree guard passed, applied/built (release+dev-tools latency_lab) and reverted cleanly (git diff --quiet passed post-revert).
retest: cloud-triage 2026-07-01: PRIME — Keep verdict, grid_rebuild p99 1020->864us with Δphase -0.053ms beyond the 4us noise floor and growth b 0.361→0.331 (scales better, not just faster at 10k); needs full 1M validation on the home rig.

title: Thread-local histogram buckets for grid_rebuild counting-sort count pass
notes: cloud triage pop=10000 seeds=11,42,99 (shared VM): Ditch. Wall p99 Δ -0.078ms (within/near wallNoise) BUT growth exponent REGRESSED b 0.361→0.559 (scales worse). Per-phase counters contaminated (stale shared target-dir zeroed candidate phase timers; raw Δphase -0.648ms is a broken-counter artifact, not a win) -> dphase recorded 0 = no trustworthy phase signal. Implemented + measured, came up short at cloud scale. Soft exclusion only, not a 1M kill.

title: L1 aggregation: reduce per cell instead of per proxy
notes: cloud triage pop=10000 seeds=11,42,99 (shared VM): Ditch. Per-phase counters INTACT for this build (trustworthy): Δphase +0.004ms (flat). Wall p99 Δ +0.074ms (regression); growth exponent REGRESSED b 0.361→0.433. No win on any trustworthy metric. Implemented + measured, came up short at cloud scale. Soft exclusion only, not a 1M kill.

title: Grid counting-sort prefix sum from scratch counts, no atomic loads
notes: cloud triage pop=10000 seeds=11,42,99 (shared VM): Ditch. Wall p99 Δ +0.164ms (regression); growth exponent b 0.361→0.345 (marginal, within noise). Per-phase counters contaminated (raw Δphase -0.706ms is a broken-counter artifact, not a win) -> dphase recorded 0 = no trustworthy phase signal. Wall regresses. Implemented + measured, came up short at cloud scale. Soft exclusion only, not a 1M kill.

title: L1 LOD: skip aggregation of cells containing only tiny entities
notes: cloud triage pop=10000 seeds=11,42,99 (shared VM): Ditch. Biological LOD — skip L1 aggregation for tiny-only cells (tradeoff: tiny entities become invisible to L1 cone queries). Wall p99 Δ +0.474ms (clear regression); growth exponent REGRESSED b 0.361→0.425. Per-phase counters contaminated (raw Δphase -0.255ms is a broken-counter artifact, not a win) -> dphase recorded 0. Implemented + measured, came up short at cloud scale. Soft exclusion only, not a 1M kill.
